problem_summary: Missing remediation template for ai:finding tickets marked as bounded_fix_l1. The existing templates (quickwin-l1.yaml, pilot/*) don't define a specific template for the remediation pipeline's bounded L1 fix path. Need a template that defines how bounded_fix_l1 flagged findings get processed.
success_criteria:
1. A new file config/task-templates/remediation-bounded-fix-l1.yaml exists
2. It follows the task template schema from README.md
3. It defines role=worker, queue_level=1, operation_type=provider
4. It includes acceptance_criteria for bounded fix verification
5. It includes verification steps that can be automated
6. It specifies allowed_paths to prevent scope creep
7. The YAML is valid and parseable
validation_cmds: python3 -c "import yaml; t=yaml.safe_load(open('config/task-templates/remediation-bounded-fix-l1.yaml')); assert t.get('name')=='remediation-bounded-fix-l1'; assert t.get('role')=='worker'; assert t.get('queue_level')==1; print('Template valid')"
output_contract: A single YAML file at config/task-templates/remediation-bounded-fix-l1.yaml. Must include: name, display_name, description, role (worker), operation_type (provider), queue_level (1), planner_prompt_template with remediation-specific instructions, acceptance_criteria, verification steps, allowed_paths, and timeout_profile (normal-45m).

compliance.sred_uncertainty: SR&ED: Automated remediation template system for AI-driven code fixes
